rabbit_holes.summary: Mass UFO sighting over Arizona witnessed by thousands, from Prescott to Tucson. Governor Fife Symington initially mocked it, then admitted years later he saw something inexplicable.
rabbit_holes.title: The Phoenix Lights — What Really Happened on March 13, 1997?

rabbit_holes.summary: From Apache Wars frontier post to the nerve center of Army intelligence. NETCOM, SIGINT, cyber warfare, and drone operations all trace back to this remote southern Arizona base.
rabbit_holes.title: Fort Huachuca — America's Intelligence Factory in the Desert

rabbit_holes.summary: The world's largest aircraft storage and regeneration facility. AMARG holds everything from B-52s to classified drones. Why Arizona's dry air preserves military history.
rabbit_holes.title: Davis-Monthan & The Boneyard — 4,400 Aircraft in the Desert Sun

rabbit_holes.summary: The Central Arizona Project, Lake Powell's decline, tribal water rights, and the political battle that will define the state's future. Follow the water.
rabbit_holes.title: Arizona Water Wars — Who Controls the Colorado River?

rabbit_holes.summary: Autonomous towers, ground sensors, drone patrols, AI-powered cameras. The AZ border is the testing ground for the most advanced surveillance infrastructure in the western hemisphere.
rabbit_holes.title: The Border Technology Corridor — Surveillance, AI, and the Wall

rabbit_holes.summary: State government breaches, election infrastructure debates, critical infrastructure targeting, and the growing cyber workforce centered around Fort Huachuca and ASU.
rabbit_holes.title: Arizona's Cyber Landscape — From Election Security to Ransomware
